content: Neon connection pooling for Next.js vs long-lived processes: Next.js API routes (serverless) → use @neondatabase/serverless neon-http driver (HTTP transport, stateless, no pool management needed): import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/neon-http'. Long-lived Node.js process (relay server Phase 2+) → use pg package with node-postgres: import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/node-postgres'. Neon provides two connection strings: 1) Direct connection string (postgres://...) for migrations and long-lived processes, 2) Pooled connection string (postgres://.../neondb?pgbouncer=true) for serverless. For Phase 1 (Next.js only), use neon-http driver for all DB access. Env vars: DATABASE_URL (direct, for migrations), DATABASE_URL_POOLED (pooled, for runtime routes). The @neondatabase/serverless package v1.1.0 supports both HTTP and WebSocket modes.
